tactical_shape: Shrewd side that never bit on Canada's Alphonso Davies decoy
4-? not specified; used key second-half changes effectively
Attacked through the left side and the back post after halftime, with Manzambi and Vargas combining in a 12-minute burst
standout_players: Ruben Vargas — scored after the restart
Johan Manzambi — scored and was rewarded for Yakin's faith
Ricardo Rodriguez — initiated the move that led to Switzerland's early second-half chance
Breel Embolo — controlled the ball and threatened early
Nico Elvedi — made a heroic block to deny Jonathan David
Gregor Kobel — saved Ali Ahmed's shot late in the first half
home_read.momentum: Impressive Nati who delivered as a team, won the group, and return to Vancouver with knockout momentum.

tactical_shape: Jesse Marsch used Alphonso Davies as a decoy, but he was never going to play
Deployed Nathan Saliba and Mathieu Choinière in midfield after Stephen Eustáquio was not fit enough to start
Looked to chase the draw after halftime, then made changes to spark a response
Relied on high energy at home in a humid, pressurised atmosphere
standout_players: Promise David — supersub who scored Canada's goal on his World Cup debut
Maxime Crépeau — raced out to deny Embolo early
Ali Ahmed — had a scuffed low shot saved by Kobel
Jonathan David — denied by Elvedi, then later made an impact with the goal assist from Saliba's touch and cross
fatigue_injuries: Alphonso Davies — not ready yet and unused
Ismaël Koné — injured, with his leg shattered earlier in the tournament
Stephen Eustáquio — not fit enough to start
Short four-day turnaround for the last-32 trip to Los Angeles
away_read.momentum: Defeat ends their Vancouver stay and sends them to Los Angeles, but Marsch insists they still have a massive chance to electrify the nation.
